old_value:
{"id":"project-LICHEN-cbqp","title":"app_identity: copy_peers returns 0 for NULL violating _Nonnull contract","description":"file: lichen/subsys/lichen/app_identity/app_identity.c:277

lichen_app_identity_copy_peers() declares its 'out' parameter as _Nonnull (header line 88-89) but returns 0U when passed NULL instead of indicating error. All other _Nonnull functions in this module return -EINVAL for NULL input.

Problem: The return type is size_t (unsigned), making it impossible to return negative error codes. Returning 0 for NULL is indistinguishable from 'no peers found', masking caller bugs.

Options:
1. Add assert(out != NULL) to crash on contract violation
2. Change signature to return int and output count via pointer
3. Accept current defensive behavior but document it clearly

Recommended: Option 1 (assert) since _Nonnull is a contract and other code in codebase uses asserts for contract violations.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T17:18:43.683473Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T17:18:43.683511Z"}

new_value: Added __ASSERT for _Nonnull contract in copy_peers(); updated test to remove NULL expectation. Matches secure_zero pattern. Zephyr test compatible. 3 codereviews passed with lints/builds clean. No deadcode/comments/style violations.
